name: VSFP-Butterfly
description: FRET-based voltage indicator with a novel 'Butterfly' topology sandwiching the voltage-sensing domain between two fluorescent proteins.

spectrum.name: VSFP-Butterfly
spectrum.note: Composite FRET spectrum: excitation is the donor mCitrine (FPbase mCitrine, verbatim — corr 1.00, peak ~516 nm), emission is the acceptor mKate2 (FPbase mKate2, peak 633 nm). VSFP-Butterfly 1.2 is the yellow–red (YR) variant — excite mCitrine, read the ratiometric mKate2/mCitrine emission. Previously the stored emission was mCitrine's own (donor, 529 nm), which omitted the far-red mKate2 acceptor. Both arrays normalized to max = 1.0.

voltage.name: VSFP-Butterfly
voltage.note: Re-digitized the black (Butterfly 1.2) min→max-normalized Boltzmann F-V of the mKate2/mCitrine ratio (Akemann 2012 Fig. 2C, ticks −140/0/+60 mV, V½ −79 mV), overlay-verified. The ratiometric ΔR/R is POSITIVE-going: the acceptor/donor ratio INCREASES with depolarization (Fig. 2A and Fig. 3B traces both deflect upward at +60 mV). Rescaled to signed ΔR/R using the paper's full-range 15% (min at −140 → max plateau; footnote: 15% is the −140→+120 mV range) with −70 mV = 0. The −70→+30 mV 100-mV step is ≈ +6% (curve plateaus by −20 mV). Previously stored negative-going, which was the wrong signal convention (donor-quench sign); corrected to match the acceptor/donor ratio actually plotted and the sibling VSFP2/VSFP2.3 ratiometric convention.

kinetics.note: Activation (on) is the amplitude-weighted mean of the double-exponential ON fit (−70→+60 mV step): τ1_on 1.0 ms (40%), τ2_on 12.2 ms (60%) → 0.40×1.0 + 0.60×12.2 = 7.7 ms (Akemann 2012 Fig. 2E/F; tabulated in Mishina 2014 fnmol Table 1). Deactivation (off, return to −70 mV) is single-exponential, 89.9 ms. Ratiometric mKate2/mCitrine response, ~32–34°C.

dynamicRangeData.note: Ratiometric ΔR/R (mKate2/mCitrine) per 100 mV (−70→+30 mV), read off the F-V (Akemann 2012 Fig. 2C). POSITIVE-going: the acceptor/donor ratio rises with depolarization. Mishina 2014 fnmol Table 1 lists R/R = 15.0 ± 0.7%, but its footnote states that is the FULL −140→+120 mV range (max−min of the ratio), NOT a 100-mV step; rescaling the digitized Fig. 2C curve to that 15% and taking the −70→+30 mV step gives ≈ +6% (the curve plateaus by −20 mV, so a 100-mV depolarizing step from −70 nearly saturates it). PC12 cells, 1P widefield. Previously stored −9 (negative sign) — wrong signal convention; corrected.

Ratiometric ΔR/R₀ (mKate2/mCitrine) for the −70→+30 mV 100-mV step in cortical brain slices (layer 2/3 pyramidal, 12 cells), read off the signed ΔR/R₀ (%) Boltzmann F-V (Akemann 2012 Fig. 3C right panel). ΔR/R₀ is referenced to the −70 mV holding potential (Fig. 3B caption: response to steps 'from a −70 mV holding potential'), so ΔR/R₀ = 0% at −70 mV by definition; V½ = −69 mV sits at that midpoint. The curve plateaus at ≈ +1.9% by +20 mV, so the −70→+30 mV step ≈ +1.9% (upper arm of the sigmoid). The paper's stated TOTAL dynamic range is 3.2 ± 0.6% (full −140→+60 mV, min ≈ −1.5% to max ≈ +2.0%, max slope 4.2%/100 mV). Attenuated vs PC12 (15%) by unlabeled-cell baseline fluorescence in the slice. POSITIVE-going (Fig. 3B ΔR/R₀ traces deflect upward at +60 mV). 1P widefield.

sensitivityData.note: Ratiometric ΔR (mKate2/mCitrine) per action potential in layer 2/3 pyramidal neurons, cortical slices (6 cells). 1P widefield.

brightnessData.source: EC×QY: mCitrine EC=77000, QY=0.76 vs EGFP EC=56000, QY=0.60

twoPhoton.note: VSFP-Butterfly 1.2 imaged under two-photon excitation in cortical slices and in vivo (Akemann et al. 2013, Sci Rep).
